title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated virtual environment and install required packages for D-HLL implementation and experiments

steps.1: Create virtual environment: python3 -m venv .venv
steps.2: Activate environment: source .venv/bin/activate
steps.3: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy scipy pandas matplotlib seaborn tabulate mmh3
steps.4: Install dev dependencies: pip install pytest pytest-benchmark
steps.5: Verify installation: python -c 'import numpy; import mmh3; print("OK")'
steps.6: Create directory structure: mkdir -p src/ data/ results/ figures/ logs/

title: Generate Synthetic Datasets
description: Create synthetic data streams with known properties for controlled evaluation

steps.1: Generate uniform random stream: 10M unique elements, timestamps 0-10000, save as data/uniform_10m.jsonl (format: {id, timestamp})
steps.2: Generate Zipfian stream: 10M elements, skew=1.5, timestamps 0-10000, save as data/zipfian_10m.jsonl
steps.3: Generate bursty stream: 5M baseline elements + 3 bursts of 1M each at t=2500, 5000, 7500, save as data/bursty_10m.jsonl
steps.4: Generate varying cardinality stream: Linear growth from 1K to 100K unique elements over 10000 timesteps, save as data/varying_100k.jsonl
steps.5: Create ground truth files: For each dataset, compute exact decayed cardinality at intervals for lambda values [0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1], save as data/*_truth_lambda-{value}.csv
steps.6: Compute dataset statistics: element counts, unique counts per time window, distribution histograms, save to data/statistics.json

title: Download and Prepare Real-World Datasets
description: Obtain real network trace data for realistic evaluation

steps.1: Download CAIDA trace sample: wget -O data/caida_sample.csv.gz 'https://data.caida.org/datasets/passive-2018/equinix-chicago/20180419-130000.UTC/anonymized.traces.gz' (or use synthetic network trace if unavailable)
steps.2: Extract and parse: gunzip and extract (src_ip, dst_ip, timestamp) tuples, limit to 5M packets
steps.3: Preprocess: Convert to element ID using hash of flow key (src_ip, dst_ip, protocol), timestamp in milliseconds
steps.4: Generate ground truth: Compute exact flow cardinality with exponential decay at regular intervals
steps.5: Create synthetic network trace fallback: If CAIDA unavailable, generate realistic network traffic with diurnal patterns and bursts

title: Implement and Evaluate Standard HLL (Landmark Model)
description: Baseline 1: Standard HyperLogLog treating all history equally

steps.1: Implement standard HLL with precision p=11 (m=2048 registers) in src/hll.py
steps.2: Implement HyperLogLog++ bias correction and 64-bit hash using mmh3
steps.3: Run on uniform stream: 3 random seeds [42, 123, 999], record relative error at t=1000, 3000, 5000, 7000, 10000
steps.4: Run on Zipfian stream: same seeds and timestamps, record relative error
steps.5: Run on bursty stream: same seeds, focus on recovery periods after bursts
steps.6: Measure throughput: elements/second using timeit with 1M elements

title: Implement and Evaluate Sliding Window HLL
description: Baseline 2: Sliding HyperLogLog with landmark windows

steps.1: Implement Sliding HLL with fixed-size landmark windows in src/sliding_hll.py
steps.2: Maintain multiple HLL sketches for different time windows (window_size = 1000 timesteps)
steps.3: Run on uniform stream with 3 seeds, measure relative error at query points
steps.4: Run on Zipfian stream with 3 seeds
steps.5: Run on bursty stream, measure accuracy during and after bursts
steps.6: Measure memory usage: total bytes for all active windows

title: Implement Decaying HyperLogLog (D-HLL)
description: Core implementation of the proposed D-HLL algorithm

steps.1: Implement D-HLL class in src/dhll.py with: registers array (float32), timestamps array (uint32), decay rate lambda
steps.2: Implement lazy decay: M[j] = M[j] * exp(-lambda * (t_now - T[j])) on register access
steps.3: Implement update: compute decayed rank, update register if new value > current
steps.4: Implement cardinality estimate: alpha_m * m^2 / sum(2^(-M[j])) using decayed register values
steps.5: Implement merge: synchronize timestamps, take max of decayed values, preserve mergeability
steps.6: Add quantized timestamp option: store timestamps at 100ms granularity to reduce space
steps.7: Unit tests: verify correctness against ground truth for small examples

title: D-HLL Accuracy vs Decay Rate
description: Evaluate accuracy across different decay rates

steps.1: Configure D-HLL with p=11 (m=2048), varying lambda in [0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1]
steps.2: For each lambda, run on uniform stream with 3 seeds [42, 123, 999]
steps.3: Query at t=1000, 3000, 5000, 7000, 10000, record relative error |estimate - truth| / truth
steps.4: Repeat for Zipfian stream
steps.5: Compute mean and std of relative error across seeds and query points
steps.6: Verify: relative error < 2% for all lambda values

title: D-HLL Throughput and Memory Evaluation
description: Measure computational performance of D-HLL

steps.1: Measure throughput: Process 10M elements, record time, compute elements/second
steps.2: Run 5 trials with different seeds, report mean and std
steps.3: Target: > 1M elements/second on single CPU core
steps.4: Measure memory: sizeof(registers) + sizeof(timestamps) in bytes
steps.5: Compare: D-HLL vs standard HLL vs sliding HLL memory usage
steps.6: Test with quantized timestamps (100ms granularity): measure space savings vs accuracy tradeoff

title: D-HLL Mergeability Evaluation
description: Verify distributed computation correctness

steps.1: Split uniform stream into 10 shards by timestamp modulo 10
steps.2: Create 10 D-HLL sketches, one per shard, with same lambda=0.01
steps.3: Merge all 10 sketches into one combined sketch
steps.4: Compare merged estimate vs centralized estimate (single sketch on full stream)
steps.5: Compute merge error: |merged - centralized| / centralized
steps.6: Repeat with 3 different random seeds for stream generation
steps.7: Verify: merge error < 10% for all cases

title: Adaptivity to Concept Drift
description: Test response to sudden cardinality changes

steps.1: Run D-HLL with lambda=0.02 on bursty stream
steps.2: Record estimates every 50 timesteps throughout stream
steps.3: Compare against sliding HLL (window=500) and standard HLL
steps.4: Measure: time to converge to new cardinality after each burst ends
steps.5: Compute average absolute error during recovery periods
steps.6: Verify: D-HLL adapts faster (lower convergence time) than sliding window
steps.7: Save time series and results to results/adaptivity_burst.json

title: Ablation: Lazy vs Eager Decay
description: Verify lazy decay does not significantly impact accuracy

steps.1: Implement eager decay variant: decay all registers at each query point
steps.2: Run both variants on uniform stream with lambda=0.01, 3 seeds
steps.3: Query at 100 points throughout stream, compare relative errors
steps.4: Compute mean absolute difference in relative error between lazy and eager
steps.5: Verify: difference < 1% (lazy decay is accurate enough)
steps.6: Measure throughput difference: lazy should be >10x faster

title: Ablation: Timestamp Quantization
description: Evaluate space-accuracy tradeoff of timestamp quantization

steps.1: Test quantization levels: 1ms (no quantization), 10ms, 100ms, 1s
steps.2: For each level, run on uniform stream with lambda=0.01, 3 seeds
steps.3: Measure: memory usage (bytes) and relative error
steps.4: Verify: 100ms quantization provides >30% space savings with <0.5% accuracy loss
steps.5: Repeat for Zipfian stream

title: Ablation: Register Count (m) Scaling
description: Verify error scales as O(1/√m)

steps.2: For each m, run D-HLL on uniform stream with lambda=0.01, 3 seeds
steps.3: Compute relative standard error across multiple query points
steps.4: Fit: error = c/√m, estimate constant c
steps.5: Verify: R² > 0.95 for O(1/√m) fit

title: Sensitivity Analysis: Hash Function Quality
description: Verify robustness to hash function choice

steps.1: Test hash functions: mmh3 (default), SHA256 truncated, simple multiplicative hash
steps.2: For each hash, run D-HLL on uniform stream, 3 seeds
steps.3: Measure relative error distribution
steps.4: Verify: all hash functions achieve < 3% relative error
steps.5: Note any significant accuracy differences

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Verify improvements are statistically significant

steps.1: Collect all relative error measurements from main experiments
steps.2: Perform paired t-test: D-HLL vs Sliding HLL on same datasets
steps.3: Perform paired t-test: D-HLL vs Standard HLL (on decayed ground truth)
steps.4: Report p-values and effect sizes (Cohen's d)
steps.5: Verify: p < 0.05 for significant differences where expected

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Check all success criteria from proposal

steps.1: Check: Relative bias < 5% across lambda ∈ [0.001, 0.1] → PASS/FAIL
steps.2: Check: Relative standard error < 2% for m=2048 → PASS/FAIL
steps.3: Check: Merge error < 10% → PASS/FAIL
steps.4: Check: Throughput > 1M elements/second → PASS/FAIL
steps.5: Check: Error scales as O(1/√m) → PASS/FAIL
steps.6: Generate summary report: results/success_criteria.json with all criteria and PASS/FAIL status
steps.7: If any FAIL: document reasons and implications

title: Generate Accuracy Comparison Figures
description: Create figures for paper showing accuracy results

steps.1: Figure 1: Relative error vs decay rate (lambda) for D-HLL, bars showing std across seeds
steps.2: Figure 2: Accuracy comparison across methods (D-HLL, Sliding HLL, Standard HLL) on uniform and Zipfian
steps.3: Figure 3: Error distribution histograms for D-HLL at different lambdas
steps.4: Figure 4: Time series of estimates vs ground truth on bursty stream
steps.5: Save all figures to figures/ as PDF (vector) and PNG (300 DPI)
steps.6: Use consistent style: seaborn whitegrid, readable fonts, clear labels

title: Generate Performance and Scaling Figures
description: Create figures for performance characteristics

steps.1: Figure 5: Throughput comparison (bar chart) across methods
steps.2: Figure 6: Memory usage comparison (bar chart), breakdown by component
steps.3: Figure 7: Error vs 1/√m (scatter + fit line) verifying O(1/√m) scaling
steps.4: Figure 8: Space-accuracy tradeoff for timestamp quantization
steps.5: Figure 9: Merge error across number of merged sketches
steps.6: Save all figures to figures/ as PDF and PNG

title: Generate Summary Tables
description: Create tables for paper with key results

steps.1: Table 1: Method comparison (accuracy, throughput, memory) with mean ± std
steps.2: Table 2: D-HLL accuracy across decay rates lambda
steps.3: Table 3: Ablation study results (lazy vs eager, quantization levels)
steps.4: Table 4: Success criteria checklist with achieved values
steps.5: Generate LaTeX tables for easy inclusion in paper
steps.6: Save tables to results/tables.txt and results/tables.tex

title: Final Report Generation
description: Compile all results into comprehensive report

steps.1: Load all result JSON files from results/
steps.2: Generate markdown report: results/EXPERIMENTS.md
steps.3: Include: experiment summaries, key findings, success criteria status
steps.4: Include: figures with captions, tables with interpretations
steps.5: Document any deviations from plan and reasons
steps.6: Provide data availability statement: all data in data/, all code in src/
